name: Claude
description: Anthropic's AI assistant known for nuanced, thoughtful responses and strong analytical capabilities. Excellent for strategy work, long-form writing, and complex analysis.
whyUseIt: Claude excels at longer, more thoughtful marketing work — writing comprehensive marketing plans, analysing competitive landscapes, crafting brand voice guidelines, and providing strategic recommendations. It handles nuance better than most AI tools.
aiCapabilities: Long-form content creation, strategic analysis, code generation, document analysis, and multi-step reasoning for complex marketing problems.
outputs: Long-form blog posts and thought leadership articles
Brand voice guidelines and tone-of-voice frameworks
Email sequence copy and campaign messaging
marketerWorkflow: A marketer opens Claude to draft campaign briefs, brainstorm positioning angles, or rewrite landing page copy with a specific tone. They paste in analytics data or competitor content and ask Claude to identify patterns, gaps, or opportunities. Throughout the day, Claude becomes a thinking partner for tasks that require strategic depth rather than quick one-liners.
managerExpects: A marketing manager expects team members to use Claude to accelerate strategic deliverables — producing first drafts of marketing plans, competitive analyses, and campaign briefs in hours instead of days. They want to see the AI used as a force multiplier for quality thinking, not a replacement for it, with all outputs reviewed and refined by the marketer before sharing.
aiWorkflowImpact: In 2025, Claude's extended context window means marketers can upload entire brand guideline documents, past campaign reports, and competitor research in a single conversation and get analysis that accounts for all of it. Projects functionality allows persistent workspaces where the AI remembers your brand context across sessions, eliminating repetitive re-prompting.
demoDescription: Open claude.ai and paste in a competitor's homepage copy. Ask Claude to analyse their positioning, identify their target audience, list their key value propositions, and suggest three ways your brand could differentiate. Then ask it to draft a one-page competitive positioning brief based on the analysis.

vocabulary.definition: The practice of crafting specific, well-structured instructions to get the best possible output from an AI model.
The maximum amount of text (measured in tokens) that the AI can process in a single conversation, determining how much information it can consider at once.
Standalone documents, code, or visualisations that Claude creates in a separate panel for easy copying and iteration.
Background instructions given to Claude that shape its behaviour, tone, and output format for a specific use case.
Persistent workspaces in Claude where you can store custom instructions and reference documents that carry across multiple conversations.
A setting that controls how creative or deterministic the AI's responses are — lower is more predictable, higher is more varied.

beginnerMistakes: Writing vague one-line prompts instead of providing detailed context, examples, and constraints
Accepting the first output without iterating — treating the AI as a vending machine rather than a collaborator
Failing to provide brand voice guidelines or existing content samples, resulting in generic-sounding output
Using Claude for real-time data or current events without verifying, since the model has a knowledge cutoff
Not breaking complex requests into steps — asking for an entire marketing plan in one prompt instead of building it section by section
